lines.text: [Scene: Central Perk, everyone is there except Joey.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Alright. Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Okay, okay. If I were omnipotent for a day, I would want, um, world peace, no more hunger, good things for the rain-forest...And bigger boobs!
lines.korean: 좋아 좋아. 내가 하루 동안 전능하다면, 세계 평화, 기아 종식, 열대우림이 잘 되는 거… 그리고 가슴이 더 커지는 거!
lines.explanation: omnipotent = 전능한(무엇이든 할 수 있는). 'If I were ~, I would ~'는 현재 사실과 반대를 가정하는 가정법 과거 — 실제론 전능하지 않으니 were/would를 쓴다.
examples: If I were rich, I would travel the world.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Yeah, see.. you took mine. Chandler, what about you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Uh, if I were omnipotent for a day, I'd.. make myself omnipotent forever.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: See, there's always one guy. (Mocking) "If I had a wish, I'd wish for three more wishes." (Joey enters.)
lines.korean: 봐, 꼭 이런 애가 하나 있어. (흉내) '소원이 하나 있으면, 소원 세 개를 더 빌 거야.'
lines.explanation: there's always one guy = '꼭 이런 사람 하나는 있다'는 관용 표현. 'If I had a wish, I'd ~'도 가정법(소원이 있다면 ~할 텐데). 소원으로 소원을 더 비는 얄미운 타입을 흉내 내는 것.
examples: There's always one in every group.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Hey Joey. Hi. Hey, buddy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Hey, Joey, what would you do if you were omnipotent?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Probably kill myself!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: ..Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Hey, if Little Joey's dead, then I got no reason to live!
lines.korean: 야, '리틀 조이'가 죽으면 난 살 이유가 없다고!
lines.explanation: 'Little Joey' = 조이가 자기 남성 부위를 의인화해 부르는 별명. impotent(발기불능)로 오해한 데서 이어지는 농담. I got no ~ = I have no ~(구어).

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Joey, uh- OMnipotent.
lines.korean: 조이, 어- '옴'니포턴트(전능한) 말이야.
lines.explanation: 로스가 단어 강세를 OM-nipotent라고 또박또박 짚는 장면. 조이가 omnipotent(전능한)를 'I'm impotent(나 발기불능이야)'로 잘못 들은 데서 시작된 말장난.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: You are? Ross, I'm sorry..
lines.korean: 너가? 로스, 안됐다…
lines.explanation: 조이가 omnipotent을 'I'm impotent(나 성불능이야)'로 알아들어, 로스가 자기 얘기를 한 줄 알고 위로하는 것. impotent = (남성) 발기불능. omnipotent(전능)와 발음이 비슷해 빚어진 오해.

lines.text: Opening Credits
[Scene: Central Perk, Ross and Monica are watching Phoebe sleep.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: How does she do that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: I cannot sleep in a public place.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Would you look at her? She is so peaceful.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: (waking and startling them) Oh! What what what! ...Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: It's okay, y'know, you just nodded off again.
lines.korean: 괜찮아, 그냥 또 깜빡 졸았던 거야.
lines.explanation: nod off = (앉은 채로) 깜빡 졸다/꾸벅꾸벅 졸다.
examples: He nodded off during the meeting.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: What's going on with you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: I got no sleep last night!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Why?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: My grandmother has this new boyfriend, and they're both kind of insecure in bed. Oh, and deaf. So they're constantly, like, having to reassure each other that they're having a good time. You have no idea how loud they are!
lines.korean: 할머니한테 새 남자친구가 생겼는데, 둘 다 잠자리에서 좀 자신이 없어. 게다가 귀도 안 들리고. 그래서 서로 좋았다고 끊임없이 안심시켜 줘야 해. 둘이 얼마나 시끄러운지 넌 상상도 못 해!
lines.explanation: insecure = (불안해서) 자신 없는. reassure = 안심시키다/다독이다. You have no idea = 넌 짐작도 못 할걸(정도 강조).

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Well, if you want, you can stay with Rachel and me tonight.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Thanks.

lines.text: (Chandler and Joey enter. Joey is counting his steps.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: ...Ninety-five, ninety-six, ninety-seven. See, I told you! Less than a hundred steps from our place to here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: You got waaaay too much free time.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: (to Ross) Hey! Here's the birthday boy! Ross, check it out: hockey tickets, Rangers-Penguins, tonight at the Garden, and we're taking you.
lines.korean: 야! 생일 주인공 등장! 로스, 이것 봐: 하키 티켓이야, 레인저스 대 펭귄스, 오늘 밤 가든에서. 너 데려갈 거야.
lines.explanation: check it out = 이것 봐봐/한번 봐. the Garden = 매디슨 스퀘어 가든(뉴욕의 대형 경기장). Rangers/Penguins = NHL(미국 프로 아이스하키) 팀.
examples: Check it out, I got new shoes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Happy birthday, pal!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: We love you, man. (Kisses Ross)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Funny, my birthday was seven months ago.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: So?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: So, I'm guessing you had an extra ticket and couldn't decide which one of you got to bring a date?
lines.korean: 그러니까, 표가 하나 남는데 너희 둘 중 누가 데이트 상대를 데려올지 못 정한 거지?
lines.explanation: get to + 동사원형 = ~할 기회·특권을 얻다, ~하게 되다. 'which one of you got to bring a date' = 너희 중 누가 데이트 상대를 '데려오는 기회'를 가질지. 표가 한 장 남아 둘 중 한 명만 데려올 수 있는 상황을 비꼬는 것.
examples: I got to meet the author.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Well, aren't we Mr. "The glass is half empty."
lines.korean: 거참, 아주 '물이 반밖에 안 남았네' 씨 나셨네.
lines.explanation: the glass is half empty = (같은 물을 두고도) 비관적으로 보다(half full이면 낙관). 'aren't we Mr. ~'는 상대를 그런 사람이라며 비꼬아 부르는 말투(누구는 아주 ~씨네).

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Oh my God, oh- is today the twentieth, October twentieth?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Oh, I was hoping you wouldn't remember.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Ohhh.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: What's wrong with the twentieth?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Eleven days before Halloween.. all the good costumes are gone?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Today's the day Carol and I first.. consummated our physical relationship. (Joey is puzzled.) Sex. ..You know what, I-I'd better pass on the game. I think I'm just gonna go home and think about my ex-wife and her lesbian lover.
lines.korean: 캐럴이랑 내가 처음… 육체적 관계를 완성한 날이야. (조이 어리둥절) 섹스 말이야. …그냥 경기는 빠지는 게 낫겠어.
lines.explanation: consummate (a relationship) = (관계를) 완성하다 — 특히 결혼·연애에서 첫 잠자리를 갖다(격식·완곡). pass on ~ = ~을 사양하다/안 하기로 하다.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: The hell with hockey, let's all do that!
lines.korean: 하키 따위 집어치우고, 우리 다 같이 그거 하자!
lines.explanation: the hell with ~ = ~ 따위 집어치워/알 게 뭐야(짜증·자포자기조의 구어).
examples: The hell with it, let's just go.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (trying to stop Ross leaving) C'mon, Ross! You, me, Joey, ice, guys' night out, c'mon, whaddya say, big guy, (Pretending to punch him in the stomach.) Huh? Huh? Huh?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (stops) I have no idea.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: C'mon, Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Alright, alright, maybe it'll take my mind off it. Do you promise to buy me a big thumb finger?
lines.korean: 알았어 알았어, 어쩌면 그게 신경을 딴 데로 돌려줄지도. 나 큰 엄지손가락 응원폼 사주기로 약속해?
lines.explanation: take one's mind off ~ = ~에서 생각을 딴 데로 돌리다(잊게 해주다). 'big thumb finger' = 경기장에서 흔드는 큰 손가락(#1) 모양 응원 소품(foam finger).
examples: A movie might take your mind off things.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: You got it.

lines.text: (Rachel runs up cluching an envelope.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Look-look-look-look-look, my first pay check! Look at the window, there's my name! Hi, me!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: I remember the day I got my first pay check. There was a cave-in in one of the mines, and eight people were killed.
lines.korean: 나도 첫 월급 받던 날 기억나. 광산 한 곳에서 갱도가 무너져서 여덟 명이 죽었거든.
lines.explanation: cave-in = (광산·갱도의) 붕괴/무너짐(동사 cave in = 무너지다). 피비 특유의 뜬금없이 음울한 농담.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Wow, you worked in a mine?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: I worked in a Dairy Queen, why?
lines.korean: 나 데어리 퀸에서 일했는데, 왜?
lines.explanation: Dairy Queen(DQ) = 미국의 소프트 아이스크림·패스트푸드 체인점. 앞서 '광산 붕괴' 얘기를 듣고 모니카가 '광산에서 일했냐'고 묻자, 피비가 '아니, 데어리 퀸'이라며 받아치는 장면.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: God, isn't this exciting? I earned this. I wiped tables for it, I steamed milk for it, and it was totally—(opens envelope)—not worth it. Who's FICA? Why's he getting all my money? I mean, what- Chandler, look at that.
lines.korean: …이거 봐. 피카(FICA)가 누구야? 왜 내 돈을 다 가져가?
lines.explanation: FICA = 미국 급여에서 떼는 사회보장·메디케어 세금(Federal Insurance Contributions Act). 명세서에 'FICA' 공제가 찍혀 있자 레이첼이 사람 이름인 줄 안 농담.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (looking) Oh, this is not that bad.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Oh, you're fine, yeah, for a first job.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: You can totally, totally live on this.
lines.korean: 이걸로 충분히, 충분히 먹고살 수 있어.
lines.explanation: live on ~ = ~으로 먹고살다/~을 생계 수단으로 삼다(돈·음식).
examples: Can you live on minimum wage?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Yeah, yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Oh, by the way, great service tonight.
lines.korean: 아 그나저나, 오늘 밤 서비스 아주 좋았어.
lines.explanation: 반어/농담 — 친구를 식당에서 손님인 척 대하며 '서비스 좋았다'고 능청 떠는 것(레이첼이 웨이트리스라). by the way = 그나저나/참고로.

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Oh! Yeah!

lines.text: (They all get their wallets out and give generous tips.)

lines.speaker: Guys
lines.english: Hockey! (They go to leave but are blocked by three of Rachel's friends, Leslie, Kiki, and Joanne. The guys pause to stare at them.) Hockey! Hockey. (The guys.)

lines.speaker: Leslie
lines.english: (looking around) Rachel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Oh my God! (Rachel, Leslie, Kiki, and Joanne all scream and hug each other.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (to Phoebe) I swear I've seen birds do this on Wild Kingdom.
lines.korean: 진짜, 새들이 'Wild Kingdom'에서 이러는 거 본 적 있어.
lines.explanation: Wild Kingdom = 미국의 유명 야생동물 다큐 TV 프로(Mutual of Omaha's Wild Kingdom). 친구들이 꺅꺅대며 끌어안는 걸 새들의 구애·과시 행동에 빗댄 것.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: What are you guys doing here?

lines.speaker: Kiki
lines.english: Well, we were in the city shopping, and your mom said you work here, aaand it's true!

lines.speaker: Joanne
lines.english: Look at you in the apron. You look like you're in a play.
lines.korean: 앞치마 두른 너 좀 봐. 무슨 연극에 나온 사람 같아.
lines.explanation: Look at you = (놀람·감탄) '얘 좀 봐/너 좀 봐'. in a play = 연극에 출연한. 레이첼이 웨이트리스 차림인 게 어색·신기하다는 뉘앙스.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: (to a pregnant Leslie) Look at you, you are so big I can't believe it!

lines.speaker: Leslie
lines.english: I know. I know! I'm a duplex.
lines.korean: 그러니까. 나 완전 복층(2세대) 집이라니까.
lines.explanation: duplex = 위아래 두 층/두 세대로 된 집. 만삭이라 배가 불러 '나 2층짜리야'라고 자기를 빗댄 농담.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: (to Joanne) So what's going on with you?

lines.speaker: Joanne
lines.english: Well, guess who my dad's making partner in his firm? (She points to herself and they all scream again.)
lines.korean: 자, 우리 아빠가 회사에서 누굴 파트너로 승진시키게요?
lines.explanation: make (someone) partner = (로펌·회계법인 등에서) ~를 파트너(공동 경영자)로 승진시키다. firm = (법률·회계 등 전문) 회사.

lines.speaker: Kiki
lines.english: And while we're on the subject of news.. (She holds up here finger to show off her engagement ring and they all scream again.)
lines.korean: 그리고 소식 얘기가 나온 김에…
lines.explanation: while we're on the subject of ~ = ~ 얘기가 나온 김에. on the subject of ~ = ~에 관하여.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: (to Monica) Look, look, I have elbows! (They scream.)

lines.text: [Scene: A Street, Chandler and Joey are kicking a can to each other.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Crosses the blue line and passes it up to Leetch! (Passes it to Joey.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Leetch spots Messier in the crease- there's the pass! (He kicks it to Ross, but Ross is staring into a shop window.)
lines.korean: 리치가 크리스에 있는 메시어를 포착- 패스 들어갑니다!
lines.explanation: crease = (아이스하키) 골 크리스 — 골문 앞에 표시된 골키퍼 전용 반원 구역. (일반적으로는 '주름·접힌 자국'이라는 뜻도 있음) Leetch·Messier는 당시 뉴욕 레인저스의 스타 선수로, 챈들러·조이가 길에서 하키 중계를 흉내 내는 장면.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: We'll take a brief time out while Messier stops to look at some women's shoes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Carol was wearing boots just like those the night that we- we first- y'know. Fact, she, uh- she never took'em off, 'cause we-we- (off Chandler's look) Sorry. Sorry.

lines.text: (They walk on. Chandler and Joey start to talk but Ross stops and whines.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Peach pit.
lines.korean: 복숭아씨네.
lines.explanation: peach pit = 복숭아씨(가운데 단단한 씨/핵). pit = (복숭아·자두·체리 등의) 굵은 씨. 로스가 길에 떨어진 복숭아씨를 보고 캐럴과의 그날 밤을 떠올리는 장면.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Yes, Bunny?
lines.korean: 응, 버니?
lines.explanation: Bunny(토끼) = 챈들러가 감상에 젖은 로스를 짐짓 다정하게/놀리듯 부르는 애칭.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: (points) Peach pit. That night we, uh- we had-

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: -Peaches?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Actually, nectarines, but basically...
lines.korean: 사실은 천도복숭아였지만, 뭐 거기서 거기지…
lines.explanation: nectarine = 천도복숭아 — 복숭아의 한 품종으로 껍질에 솜털이 없고 매끈하다. (조이가 'Peaches?'라고 묻자 로스가 '정확히는 천도복숭아'라고 정정하는 것)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (to Joey) Could've been a peach.
lines.korean: 복숭아였을 수도 있지, 뭐.
lines.explanation: could have + 과거분사 = (과거에) ~였을 수도 있다(추측·가정). 천도복숭아든 복숭아든 상관없다는 챈들러의 시큰둥한 한마디.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Then, uh, then we got dressed, and I-I... I walked her to the- (looks up, realises, and points) -the bus stop... I'm fine.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Hey, that woman's got an ass like Carol's! (They turn to stare at him.) What? Thought we were trying to find stuff.

lines.text: [Scene: Central Perk, Rachel, Lesile, Kiki, and Joanne are talking.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: So c'mon, you guys, tell me all the dirt!
lines.korean: 자 얘들아, 비하인드 이야기 다 풀어봐!
lines.explanation: dirt = (구어) 소문·뒷이야기·험담거리. 'tell me the dirt' = 흥미로운 뒷얘기 다 말해줘.
examples: Come on, give me the dirt on him.

lines.speaker: Kiki
lines.english: Well, the biggest news is still you dumping Barry at the altar!

lines.speaker: Joanne
lines.english: Alright. Let's talk reality for a second.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joanne
lines.english: When are you coming home?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: What? Guys, I'm not.

lines.speaker: Joanne
lines.english: C'mon, this is us.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: I'm not! This is what I'm doing now. I've got this job-

lines.speaker: Kiki
lines.english: Waitressing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Okay, I'm not just waitressing. I'm.. I, um... I write the specials on the specials board, and, uh... and I, uh... I take the uh dead flowers out of the vase... Oh, and, um, sometimes Artelle lets me put the little chocolate blobbies on the cookies.
lines.korean: …오늘의 메뉴를 칠판에 적고, 꽃병에서 시든 꽃을 빼고… 가끔 아르텔이 쿠키에 작은 초콜릿 방울을 짜 얹게 해줘.
lines.explanation: blob = (액체·반죽의) 방울/덩어리 → 'blobbies'는 거기에 귀여운 어미를 붙인 즉흥 조어(작은 초콜릿 방울). specials board = 오늘의 특선 메뉴를 적는 칠판.

lines.speaker: Leslie
lines.english: Well. Your mom didn't tell us about the blobbies.

lines.text: [Scene: Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe and Monica are in pajamas and Monica is making something in the blender as Rachel enters.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Hey, Rach. How was it with your friends? (She and Phoebe scream.) Okay! How would you like some Tiki Death Punch? (She pours the contents of the blender into some glasses.)
lines.korean: 음- 그게, 럼주에다-
lines.explanation: punch = 과일주스에 술을 섞은 파티 음료(여기선 Tiki Death Punch라는 럼주 칵테일). rum = 럼주.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: What's that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Weeeell, it's rum, and-

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Okay. (Grabs the blender and starts to drink.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: We thought since Phoebe was staying over tonight we'd have kinda like a slumber party thing. We got some trashy magazines, we got cookie dough, we got Twister... (The phone rings and Monica answers it.)
lines.korean: …일종의 파자마 파티 같은 거 하려고. 가십 잡지도 있고, 쿠키 반죽도 있고, 트위스터도 있어…
lines.explanation: slumber party = (주로 여자아이들의) 파자마 파티(친구 집에서 자며 노는 모임). Twister = 매트의 색 점에 손발을 올리며 몸이 얽히는 보드게임. trashy magazine = 가십·연예 위주의 싸구려 잡지.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Ooh! Ooh! And I brought Operation! But, um, I lost the tweezers, so we can't operate. But we can prep the guy!
lines.korean: 그리고 나 'Operation'(수술 게임) 가져왔어! 근데 핀셋을 잃어버려서 수술은 못 해. 그래도 환자 준비는 시킬 수 있어!
lines.explanation: Operation = 핀셋으로 환자 모형의 부위를 집어내는 보드게임. prep (the patient) = (수술 전) 환자를 준비시키다(prepare의 줄임, 의료 구어).

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Uh, Rach, it's the Visa card people.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Oh, God, ask them what they want.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (on phone) Could you please tell me what this is in reference to? (Listens) Yes, hold on. (To Rachel) Um, they say there's been some unusual activity on your account.
lines.korean: 이게 무슨 일로 연락 주신 건지 말씀해 주시겠어요?… 계좌에 평소와 다른 사용 내역이 있대.
lines.explanation: in reference to ~ = ~에 관하여/~ 건으로(격식). unusual activity on your account = 계좌의 평소와 다른 사용 내역(카드사·은행의 상투적 표현).

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: But I haven't used my card in weeks!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: That is the unusual activity. Look, they just wanna see if you're okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: They wanna know if I'm okay. Okay.. they wanna know if I'm okay, okay, let's see. Well, let's see, the FICA guys took all my money, everyone I know is either getting married, or getting promoted, or getting pregnant, and I'm getting coffee! And it's not even for me! So if that sounds like I'm okay, okay, then you can tell them I'm okay, okay?
lines.korean: …피카가 내 돈 다 가져가지, 아는 사람들은 죄다 결혼하거나 승진하거나 임신하는데, 난 커피나 나르고 있어! 그것도 내가 마실 것도 아니고!
lines.explanation: 'getting married/promoted/pregnant'(다들 뭔가 이루는데)와 'getting coffee'(난 커피 심부름이나)를 대비시킨 말장난·자조. get coffee = 커피를 가져다주다(심부름).

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (pauses then on the phone) Uh- Rachel has left the building, can you call back?
lines.korean: 어- 레이첼은 자리 비웠어요, 다시 걸어 주실래요?
lines.explanation: 'Elvis has left the building(엘비스는 떠났습니다)'을 빗댄 표현 — 원래 공연 후 '이제 다 끝났으니 돌아가라'는 안내 문구. 여기선 레이첼이 폭발한 뒤라 '레이첼은 (제정신이) 나갔다'는 농담.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Alright, c'mon! (Miserably) Let's play Twister!

lines.text: [Scene: Madison Square Garden, the guys are trying to find their seats.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: (squeezing past people) Sorry, sorry... Uh-oh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: What? There was ice there that night with Carol? Plastic seats? Four thousand angry Pittsburgh fans?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: No, actually I was just saying it looks like we're not sitting together. But now you mention it, there was ice there that night... It was the first frost...
lines.korean: …그날 밤에도 얼음이 있었지… 첫 서리가 내린 날이었거든…
lines.explanation: frost = 서리. the first frost = 그해 처음 내린 서리(첫 서리). 하키장 얼음을 보고 또 캐럴과의 추억을 떠올리는 로스.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: C'mon, sit. Just sit down, sit.

lines.text: [Scene: Monica and Rachel's, they're all hanging out in the living room.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: You should feel great about yourself! You're doing this amazing independent thing!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Monica, what is so amazing? I gave up, like, everything. And for what?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: You are just like Jack.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: ...Jack from downstairs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: No, Jack and the Beanstalk.
lines.korean: 아니, '잭과 콩나무'(동화)의 잭.
lines.explanation: Jack and the Beanstalk = 영미권 전래동화 '잭과 콩나무'(소를 팔아 얻은 마법의 콩에서 거대한 콩나무가 자람).

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Ah, the other Jack.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Yeah, right! See, he gave up something, but then he got those magic beans. And then he woke up, and there was this, this big plant outside his window, full of possibilities and stuff.. And he lived in a village, and you live in the Village..
lines.korean: …잭은 시골 마을(village)에 살았고, 너는 '빌리지(the Village)'에 살잖아…
lines.explanation: magic beans = (동화 속) 마법의 콩 → 여기선 '포기 대신 얻은 가능성'의 비유로 계속 등장. 말장난: village(시골 마을) ↔ the Village = 뉴욕 맨해튼의 그리니치 빌리지(레이첼이 사는 동네).

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Okay, but Pheebs, Pheebs, Jack gave up a cow, I gave up an orthodontist. Okay, I-I-I know, I know I didn't love him-

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Oh, see, Jack did love the cow.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: But see, it was a plan. Y'know, it was clear. It was figured out, and now everything's just kinda like...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Floopy?
lines.korean: '플루피'한 느낌?
lines.explanation: floopy = 정식 단어가 아니라 피비가 즉흥으로 만든 말 — '뒤죽박죽이고 붕 떠서 갈피 못 잡는' 기분. 바로 뒤 [136]에서 '그게 무슨 단어냐'고 놀림받는다.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: So what, you're not the only one. I mean, half the time we don't know where we're going. You've just gotta figure at some point it's all gonna come together, and it's just gonna be... un-floopy.
lines.korean: …언젠가는 다 맞아떨어질 거라고 생각해야지, 그럼 그게… 안 '플루피'해지는 거고.
lines.explanation: come together = (일·계획이) 잘 맞아떨어지다/정리되다. figure (that) ~ = ~라고 생각하다(짐작하다). un-floopy = 피비의 조어 floopy에 un-을 붙인 즉흥 반대말.
examples: Don't worry, it'll all come together.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Oh, like that's a word.
lines.korean: 어, 그게 무슨 단어라고.
lines.explanation: 'Like that's a word.'는 반어 — '그게 단어이긴 하겠다(=아니잖아)'라며 비꼬는 말투. 모니카가 'un-floopy'라고 받자 피비가 자기 조어를 셀프 디스하듯 받아침.
examples: Oh, like that's ever going to happen.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Okay, but Monica, what if- what if it doesn't come together?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: ...Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Oh, well... 'cause.... you just... I don't like this question.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Okay, see, see, you guys, what if we don't get magic beans? I mean, what if all we've got are.. beans?

lines.text: [Scene: Madison Square Garden, the guys are watching the game.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Get him! GET HIM! Get him! Get- YESSS! Not laughing now, are ya pal!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (to Ross) See buddy, that's all you need, a bunch of toothless guys hitting each other with sticks.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Pass it! Pass it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: He's open!

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Shoot! Shoot! Shoot!

lines.text: (The player shoots and the puck flies off the rink and hits Ross in the face. Chandler looks concered until he notices...)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Hey, look, we're on that TV thing!

lines.text: (Chandler and Joey hold the puck and wave at the TV thing.)
Commercial Break
[Scene: An Emergency Room, Chandler and Joey are leading Ross in.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: (to the receptionist)'Scuse me.

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.english: (holds up her hand—she is on the phone) It says to call this number if you're not completely satisfied with this candy bar. Well, I'm not completely satisfied.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Listen, it's kind of an emergency. Well, I guess you know that, or we'd be in the predicament room. (The receptionist glares at him.)
lines.korean: …이거 좀 응급이거든요. 뭐, 그쪽도 알겠죠, 안 그럼 우린 '곤경실'에 있겠죠.
lines.explanation: 응급실 emergency room(ER)을 비틀어 predicament room('곤경실')이라 한 말장난. predicament = 곤경·난처한 상황(emergency보다 가벼운 말).

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.english: (on phone) Hold on. (To Chandler) Fill these out, sit over there. (Tosses him some forms.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: (jumping to his feet) Look, I don't wanna make any trouble, okay, but I'm in a lot of pain here, alright? My face is dented.
lines.korean: …나 지금 엄청 아프거든요. 얼굴이 움푹 들어갔다고요.
lines.explanation: dented = 움푹 들어간/찌그러진(dent = 움푹 들어간 자국; 동사로 찌그러뜨리다). 하키 퍽에 맞아 얼굴이 들어갔다는 과장.

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.english: Well, you'll have to wait your turn.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Well, how long do you think it'll be?

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.english: (sarcastic) Any minute now.
lines.korean: (비꼬며) 이제 곧이요.
lines.explanation: any minute now = 금방이라도/이제 곧. 여기선 접수원이 빈정대며 '글쎄, 곧 되겠죠'라는 식으로 (사실 한참 걸린다는) 비꼼.
examples: The bus should be here any minute now.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Hey, this- (she gives him a look and the guys back off) Heyy...

lines.text: [Scene: Monica and Rachel's, the slumber party continues.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: I'm so sorry, you guys. I didn't mean to bring you down.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: No, you were right. I don't have a plan. (There's a knock on the door.)

lines.speaker: Pizza Guy
lines.english: (yelling from outside) Pizza guy!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Thank God. Food. (She goes to answer the door.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Do you have a plan?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: I don't even have a 'pl'.
lines.korean: 난 '플(pl)' 자도 없어.
lines.explanation: plan(계획)의 앞 두 글자 'pl'조차 없다 = 계획 근처도 못 갔다는 말장난. 모니카의 'Do you have a plan?'을 받아침.

lines.speaker: Pizza Guy
lines.english: Hi, one, uh, mushroom, green pepper and onion?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: (miserably) No, no, that's not what we ordered... We ordered a fat-free crust with extra cheese.

lines.speaker: Pizza Guy
lines.english: Wait, you're not 'G.Stephanopoulos?' Man, my dad's gonna kill me!
lines.korean: 잠깐, 'G. 스테파노풀로스' 아니에요? 아 아빠한테 죽었다!
lines.explanation: George Stephanopoulos = 빌 클린턴 대통령의 백악관 참모로 유명했던 실존 인물(이 에피소드 제목의 그 사람). 'My dad's gonna kill me' = (혼날까 봐) 아빠한테 죽었다(과장).

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (leaping off of the couch and runs up) Wait! Did you say 'G.Stephanopoulos?'

lines.speaker: Pizza Guy
lines.english: Yeah. This one goes across the street, I must have given him yours. Oh, bonehead, bonehead!
lines.korean: …아, 멍청이, 이 돌대가리!
lines.explanation: bonehead = 멍청이·돌대가리(bone+head). 배달부가 주문을 바꿔 준 자기를 자책하는 말.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Wait, was this a-a small mediterranean guy with curiously intelligent good looks?

lines.speaker: Pizza Guy
lines.english: Yeah, that sounds about right.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Was he wearing a stunning blue suit?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: And-and a power tie?

lines.speaker: Pizza Guy
lines.english: No, pretty much just a towel.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: (staggered) Oh God.

lines.speaker: Pizza Guy
lines.english: So you guys want me to take this back?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Are you nuts?! We've got George Stephanopoulos' pizza! (Rachel pays him, Monica grabs some binoculars, and runs to the window.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Uh, Pheebs? Who's George Snuffalopagus?
lines.korean: 어, 피비? 조지 '스너팔로파거스'가 누구야?
lines.explanation: 레이첼이 Stephanopoulos를 'Snuffleupagus'로 잘못 들음 — Snuffleupagus는 '세서미 스트리트'에 나오는 매머드 같은 캐릭터.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Big Bird's friend.
lines.korean: 빅버드 친구잖아.
lines.explanation: Big Bird = '세서미 스트리트'의 노란 큰 새 캐릭터. 피비도 Stephanopoulos를 세서미 캐릭터 Snuffleupagus(빅버드의 친구)로 착각해 맞장구.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: I see pizza!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Oh, I wanna see! Lemme see! Lemme see! (She runs up and takes the binoculars.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Hello? Who are we spying on?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: White House adviser? Clinton's campaign guy? The one with the great hair, sexy smile, really cute butt?
lines.korean: 백악관 참모? 클린턴 선거 참모? 머릿결 좋고, 미소 섹시하고, 엉덩이도 귀여운 그 사람?
lines.explanation: 조지 스테파노풀로스가 누군지 짚는 대사 — 1992년 클린턴 대선 캠프·백악관 참모로 이름을 알린 인물(모니카가 흠모하는 설정).

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Oh, him, the little guy? Oh, I love him!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Ooh, wait.. wait, I see a woman.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Please tell me it's his mother.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Definitely not his mother.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Oh, no...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Oh, wait, she's walking across the floor.. she's walking.. she's walking.. she's going for the pizza- (Yelling) Hey, that's not for you, bitch! (Phoebe covers her mouth with her hand walks away from the window.)

lines.text: [Scene: The Emergency Room, Joey is miming hockey pucks kitting foreheads. Chandler realises it's getting tense and goes to the receptionist again.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Excuse me, look, we've been here for over an hour, and a lot of people less sick than my friend have gone in. I mean, that guy with the toe thing? Who's he sleeping with? (She slides the gladd panel over and Chandler talks through it in a loud voice.) Oh, c'mon Dora, don't be mad... I know we both said some things we didn't mean, but that doesn't mean we still don't love each other. (To the waiting room.) Y'know, I feel like I've lost her.. (She slides the panel back, he turns, and it takes him by surprise.) Ba-!

lines.text: [Scene: Monica and Rachel's, the girls are all out on the balcony.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Light still out?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Oh. Maybe they're- napping.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Oh please, they're having sex.

lines.speaker: Monica and Phoebe
lines.english: Shut up!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: So, whaddya think George is like?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: I think he's shy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Yeah. I think you have to draw him out. And then- when you do- he's a preppy animal.
lines.korean: 그 사람은 (마음을) 끌어내 줘야 할 것 같아. 그러고 나면- 완전 프레피 야수지.
lines.explanation: draw someone out = (말수 적은 사람의) 마음을 열게 하다/속을 끌어내다. preppy = 명문 사립고 스타일의(단정·보수적인 옷차림·분위기). 'preppy animal' = 겉은 단정한데 속은 야수 같다는 농담.
examples: She's shy, but a good question can draw her out.

lines.text: [Scene: The Emergency Room, Ross is still going on about his first night with Carol.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: I remember the moonlight coming through the window- and her face had the most incredible glow.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Yes, the moon, the glow, the magical feeling, you did this part- Could I get some painkillers over here, please?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: He's right, enough, already. What is the big deal about today? So you slept with her for the first time, so what? You slept with her for seven years after that.
lines.korean: …오늘이 뭐가 그렇게 대수야? 그래, 그 여자랑 처음 잤다 치자, 그래서 뭐? 그 뒤로 7년이나 같이 잤잖아.
lines.explanation: so what? = 그래서 뭐/어쩌라고(대수롭지 않다는 반문). big deal = 대단한 일(다른 화에서도 다룸).

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Look, it's just a little more complicated...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Well, what? What? What is it? That she left you? That she likes women? That she left you for another woman that likes women?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Little louder, okay, I think there's a man on the twelfth floor in a coma that didn't quite hear you...
lines.korean: 좀 더 크게 말하지 그래, 12층에 혼수상태인 사람이 못 들은 것 같으니까…
lines.explanation: 반어 — 챈들러가 비밀을 너무 큰 소리로 떠들자 '저 위층 혼수상태 환자도 들리게 더 크게 말해라'라고 비꼬는 것. in a coma = 혼수상태인.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Then what?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: My first time with Carol was... (He mumbles the last part)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: It was my first time.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: With Carol? (Ross gives him a look.) Oh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: So in your whole life, you've only been with one—(He gets a look too)—oh.
lines.korean: 그럼 평생 너 딱 한 명이랑만— 아.
lines.explanation: be with someone = (완곡) ~와 잠자리를 갖다/연인 관계를 맺다. 여기선 로스가 평생 캐럴 한 사람하고만 관계를 가졌다는 걸 챈들러가 뒤늦게 알아챔.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Whoah, boy, hockey was a big mistake! There was a whole bunch of stuff we could've done tonight!
lines.korean: 와, 진짜, 하키는 큰 실수였어! 오늘 밤 (그거 말고) 할 수 있었던 게 엄청 많았는데!
lines.explanation: a whole bunch of ~ = 엄청 많은 ~(구어). could have done = ~할 수 있었는데(못 한 일에 대한 아쉬움, 가정법 과거완료).

lines.text: [Scene: Monica and Rachel's, the girls are still out on the balcony.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Okay. Okay, I got one. Do you remember that vegetarian pate that I made that you loved so much?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Well, unless goose is a vegetable...ha haaaah!
lines.korean: …거위가 채소가 아니라면 말이지… 하하하!
lines.explanation: vegetarian pate(채식 파테)인 줄 알고 좋아했는데 사실은 goose(거위)로 만든 거였다는 폭로 — '거위가 채소라면 모를까'라며 비꼬는 것. pate = 고기·간을 곱게 갈아 만든 프랑스식 스프레드.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Oh! Oh! Oh! Okay, fine, fine. Now I don't feel so bad about sleeping with Jason Hurley.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: What?! You slept with Jason?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: You'd already broken up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: How long?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: A couple hours.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Oh, that's nice!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Okay, okay, okay, I got one! (She sits up and the cushion she was leaning against falls off of the balcony.) Anyway- The valentine Tommy Rollerson left in your locker was really from me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Excuse me?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Hello? Like he was really gonna send you one? (To Phoebe) She was a big girl.
lines.korean: 뭐래? 걔가 정말 너한테 (밸런타인을) 보냈겠어? (피비에게) 쟤 덩치 컸거든.
lines.explanation: 'Like he was really gonna ~?' = '걔가 정말 ~했겠어?'라는 반어(아니라는 뜻). big girl = 여기선 '덩치 큰 애'(다음 줄에서 '다 큰 애'라는 뜻과 엮여 말다툼으로 번짐).

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Really. Well, at least 'big girls' don't pee in their pants in seventh grade!
lines.korean: 그러셔. 적어도 '다 큰 애들'은 7학년 때 바지에 오줌은 안 싸거든!
lines.explanation: big girl의 다른 뜻('다 큰 애/어른스러운 애')을 받아쳐서 레이첼의 어릴 적 실수를 들춰 반격하는 말. seventh grade = (미국) 7학년(중학교 1학년 무렵).

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: I was laughing! You made me laugh! (Monica and Rachel start to squabble)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: There he is! There he is!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Where?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.english: Right- where we've been looking all night!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: He is so cute!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Oh, George, baby, drop the towel!

lines.speaker: All
lines.english: Yeah, drop it! Drop the towel! Please drop the—(pause)—wowww.

lines.text: [Scene: The Emergency Room, Ross is absent.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Man. Can you believe he's only had sex with one woman?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: I think it's great. Y'know, it's sweet, it's romantic...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Really?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: No, you kidding? The guy's a freak.. (Ross enters off camera)
lines.korean: 아니, 농담해? 그 사람 완전 별종이야.
lines.explanation: freak = 별종·괴짜(정상에서 벗어난 사람). (you) kidding? = 농담해?/말이 돼?(= Are you kidding?). 앞에선 멋지다더니 곧장 뒤집는 챈들러식 반전.

lines.speaker: Both
lines.english: Hey, buddy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Hi. (He is wearing a piece of steel bandaged to his nose. He tosses some forms onto reception desk.)

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.english: (sarcastic) Oh, that's attractive.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Oh, I thought you were great in Silence of the Lambs. Oh come on, admit it! All things considered, you had fun tonight.
lines.korean: 오, 너 '양들의 침묵'에서 연기 좋더라. …이래저래 따져 보면, 오늘 밤 재밌었잖아.
lines.explanation: Silence of the Lambs = 영화 '양들의 침묵' — 로스의 코 보호대를 한니발 렉터의 얼굴 마스크에 빗댄 농담. all things considered = 이래저래 따져 보면/모든 걸 감안하면.
examples: All things considered, the trip went well.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Fun? Where was the fun? Tell me specifically, which part was the fun part? Where's my puck?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.english: Oh, ah- the kid has it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: The kid...? (To the kid) Excuse me, uh, that's, that's my puck.

lines.speaker: Kid
lines.english: I found it. Finders keepers, losers weepers. (Ross looks at Chandler for help.)
lines.korean: 내가 주웠어. 주운 사람이 임자, 잃은 사람은 우는 거야.
lines.explanation: Finders keepers, losers weepers = 영미권 아이들의 관용구 — '주운 사람이 갖고, 잃은 사람은 울 뿐'(주운 게 임자라는 뜻).

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: You gotta do it, man.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: (to the kid) Oh yeah? Well, I'm rubber, you're glue, whatever—(to Chandler)—can't do it. (to the kid) Listen, uh- gimme back my puck.
lines.korean: …그래? 난 고무고 넌 풀이야, 네가 뭐라든— (챈들러에게) 못 하겠어…
lines.explanation: 'I'm rubber, you're glue...' = 영미권 아이들의 받아치기 구절 — 상대의 욕이 나(고무)한텐 튕겨 나가 너(풀)한테 들러붙는다는 뜻. 로스가 유치한 말다툼에 끼어들다 끝까지 못 함.

lines.speaker: Kid
lines.english: No.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: 'Yes', how about. C'mere. Gimme!

lines.speaker: Kid
lines.english: No! No! (They start to fight over it.)

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.english: Hey! Hey! No rough holding in my ER!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: (tries to snatch it from the kid) GIVE ME MY PUCK!! (but it files out of his grasp and knocks out the receptionist)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: ...Now that was fun.

lines.text: Closing Credits
[Scene: Monica and Rachel's, Joey and the girls are playing twister.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: (Doing the spinning) Okay, Monica: Right foot red.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.english: Could've played Monopoly, but nooooo.
lines.korean: 그냥 모노폴리나 할 수도 있었는데, 아니나 다를까…
lines.explanation: Monopoly = 부동산을 사고파는 미국의 대표 보드게임. 'but nooooo' = '근데 (굳이) 아니래'라며 못마땅함을 길게 빼는 말투.

lines.text: (There's a knock on the door, Chandler opens it, and silently hands back the cushion.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Thanks. (The guy nods and leaves)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Okay, Pheebs: Right hand blue. (Phoebe has to bend over.)Good. (Joey stares at her butt appreciatively)

lines.text: (The phone rings and Chandler answers it.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Hello? Oh, uh, Rachel, it's the Visa card people.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Oh, okay. Will you take my place?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: Alright. (on phone) Yes, this is Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: Nooo! (She grabs the phone and Chandler takes her place on the mat.) (On phone) Hello? (Listens) Oh, yeah, no, I know, I-I haven't been using it much. (Listens) Oh, well, thanks, but, I'm okay, really.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.english: Green. To the green.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: (on phone) I've got magic beans. (Listens) Never-never mind.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.english: To the left, to the left- aww! (They all collapse)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.english: (on phone) Ohhh... I'm fine.

lines.text: End
